statement: The system shall provide a baseline configuration that can be extended with longer arms, higher lift capacity, or waterproofing as required by a project.
The system shall enter safe mode when a movement command exceeds the programmed capabilities or the internal processing speed.
The system shall display the range of each joint on the MMI screens and change the display colour when a joint approaches its limits.
The system shall provide stops or guides to ensure the cable remains within the designated area.
The system shall allow programming of autonomous point-to-point navigation without manual joystick control.
The system shall allow programming the arm to move to a specified position and provide a home button that returns the arm to a predefined state.
The system shall move autonomously to a position near the task and then transfer control to the operator.
The system shall provide feedback to indicate when an operation is proceeding incorrectly.
The system shall provide a variable viewpoint that allows users to look around to enhance depth perception.
The system shall provide a fixed camera view that can be selected and remain constant.
The system shall prioritize resolution over frame rate.
The system shall use force feedback to indicate forward motion and stop movement when forward motion is not detected.
The system shall allow the user to activate ghost mode via a button to display the predicted final position of the object.
The system shall prevent users from entering designated no-go zones.
The system shall provide an alarm when drifting into a no-go zone.
The system shall recognize materials by scanning and identify rubber, aluminium, painted surfaces, and other material types.
The system shall use material recognition to define no-go zones represented by colored blocks to assist instruction.
